---
title: "tbox-finder тАФ Phase 0 (Foundation) dev-log"
author: "bioedca"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Phase 0 тАФ Foundation

Per-step development log (CLAUDE.md ┬з2.4). One stanza is appended after every
step's commit; this file renders to `docs/dev-log/phase0_<YYYY-MM-DD>.pdf` at
the phase-exit gate.

## Step P0-01 тАФ 2026-07-07: Scaffold the repository to the PRD ┬з16 layout

**Inputs.** PRD ┬з16 layout spec.
**Process.** Created the ┬з16 directory tree (`src/tbox_finder/`,
`workflow/{rules,profiles/slurm}`, `conf/{model,data,optim}`, `envs/`,
`slurm/`, `data/{raw,external,interim,processed}/`,
`tests/{unit,golden,ml,fixtures}/`, `analyses/`, `figures/`, `paper/`, `app/`,
`docker/`, `docs/decisions/`); authored `pyproject.toml` (setuptools src-layout
+ ruff/black/pytest config), a glob-including `workflow/Snakefile` with an empty
default `rule all`, `src/tbox_finder/__init__.py`, a `README.md` stub, and
`.gitkeep` markers in every empty leaf dir. `data/{raw,external}` are immutable
(`.gitkeep` only).
**Outputs.** Package skeleton importable as `tbox_finder`;
`tests/unit/test_import.py`, `tests/unit/test_layout.py`.
**Validation.** `PYTHONPATH=src python -c "import tbox_finder"` тЬУ; `pytest
tests/unit` green (2 files); layout-presence test тЬУ. `snakemake -n` **deferred**
to P0-05 (pinned env) / CI P0-08 тАФ snakemake is not installable under the ┬з3.1
env-flow before the first env exists (P0-05).
**Decisions.** Ran **in place**: P0-01 is pre-git-init тАФ P0-02 performs `git
init` + the first commit тАФ so no worktree/commit/CodeRabbit at this step
(drive-one-step: pre-git-init steps run in place; the P0-01 scaffold lands in
P0-02's first commit). No ADR.
**Commit.** deferred to P0-02 (first commit).

## Step P0-02 тАФ 2026-07-07: Initialize git, publish the public GitHub repo, wire ignore/attributes/CodeRabbit

**Inputs.** P0-01 scaffold tree (working dir, no prior `.git`).
**Process.** `git init -b main` + repo-local `git lfs install`; authored
`.gitignore` (local-only docs `PRD/CLAUDE/imp/TODO/dev-manual.md`, `.dvc/config`,
`data/raw|external/**` with `.gitkeep` kept via negation, `**/wandb/`, secrets,
and a data-blob guard that deliberately spares `*.parquet`/`*.fa`/`*.fasta`/`*.cm`
since those are DVC- or git-LFS-managed), `.gitattributes` (LF normalization for
golden-hash reproducibility + git-LFS on `figures/**`, `*.cm`, `*.fa`, `*.fasta`,
`data/processed/splits/split_assignments.parquet`; `**/.gitkeep` kept plain),
and `.coderabbit.yaml` (review scoped to code-that-runs; `!`-glob `path_filters`
exclude `*.md`/`*.qmd`/ADRs/data/figures). The bootstrap first commit lands on
`main` тАФ the "empty repo can't PR into itself" exception (CLAUDE.md ┬з5.0); all
later code goes via worktree+PR. context7 (plugin server; the claude.ai server
was monthly-quota-exhausted) confirmed the `.coderabbit.yaml` v2 schema
(`reviews.path_filters`, `!`-prefixed excludes); git-lfs 3.5.1 installed to
`~/.local/bin` as a VCS dev tool (outside the `envs/*` lock flow, ┬з3.1).
**Outputs.** `.gitignore`, `.gitattributes`, `.coderabbit.yaml`; git repo on
`main`; `origin` = `https://github.com/bioedca/tbox-finder.git` (**public**).
**Validation.** Local gates тЬУ тАФ `git check-ignore` matches all 5 local-only
docs; `git lfs track` lists 5 LFS patterns (+ `**/.gitkeep` excluded); zero
binaries staged (largest blob 2.4 KB). Push gates тЬУ тАФ `gh api user -q .login`
= **bioedca**; repo visibility = **public** (verified this session). CodeRabbit
zero-finding prose-diff gate: **pending the first PR** (Stop, CLAUDE.md ┬з7).
**Decisions.** No `LICENSE` file authored тАФ README asserts code=MIT but the
copyright holder's legal name is unresolved; deferred rather than fabricate a
legal holder (┬з10.3 no-fabrication spirit). No ADR.
**Commit.** (this commit тАФ the repository's first).

## Step P0-03 тАФ 2026-07-08: Author ADR-0001 тАФ Architecture & aims (the contract ADR)

**Inputs.** PRD ┬з1, ┬з2 (esp. ┬з2.3), ┬з5, ┬з6, ┬з18.3.
**Process.** Authored `docs/decisions/ADR-0001-architecture-and-aims.md` тАФ the
first ADR and the *contract* ADR, written first so every later seed ADR + gate
step inherits the locked precedence + terminal-outcome map. It locks D1 the
two-stage Caduceus-PSтЖТRiNALMo architecture (8-class identifiers, sequence-only
Stage-2, two Stage-1 checkpoints); D2 primary/secondary aims + G-AтАжG-E; D3
claim & gate precedence (GATE-1 central + precondition for GATE-3; GATE-3 split
method-sub-gate/breadth-floor; calibrated-negative PASS); D4 the precedence
carve-out reproduced **verbatim** from ┬з2.3; D5 GATE-1-failure parity-segmenter
+ P4тЖТP5 go/no-go; D6 the **three distinct failure branches** (B1 outrightтЖТparity;
B2 underpoweredтЖТ┬з7 halt, re-power/resource-paper; B3 GATE-4/GATE-2 method-gateтЖТ
ADR-0002 NT-multispecies/continued-pretraining backbone fallbacks first); D7 the
project-level rollup + discovery-predominantly-inconclusive terminal outcome
(rollup function pinned in ADR-0006); D8 intended-use posture; D9 release-license
gating (code MIT + weights CC-BY-4.0 pinned per PRD header; dataset license
delegated to the P0 audit); D10 delegations to ADR-0002тАж0006 (no values pinned
here). Pins **no threshold values** (all delegated). Adversarial faithfulness
sweep (5 parallel verifiers over the PRD sections) caught one fabrication тАФ an
early D9 draft wrongly framed the *code* license as unresolved; corrected to the
PRD-pinned **code = MIT**, reserving the P0-audit delegation for the *dataset*
license only.
**Outputs.** `docs/decisions/ADR-0001-architecture-and-aims.md` (+ its embedded
cross-reference impact list).
**Validation.** All three failure branches stated distinctly тЬУ; precedence
carve-out verbatim vs PRD ┬з2.3 тЬУ; adversarial faithfulness sweep clean after the
license fix тЬУ; **user sign-off recorded 2026-07-08** тЬУ (CLAUDE.md ┬з7 item 2).
CodeRabbit zero-finding prose-diff gate: on this PR (prose-only ADR тЖТ expected
zero findings, correct scoping not a missing review).
**Decisions.** ADR-0001 pins no numbers by design тАФ delegation + the ┬з2.3
precedence carve-out keep every threshold authoritative in its owning ADR and
blinded-frozen at P0. Cross-ref ADR-0001.
**Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-03 atomic commit on branch `p0-03-adr-0001`).

## Step P0-04 тАФ 2026-07-08: Author ADR-0002 тАФ Environment & ML stack

**Inputs.** PRD ┬з3.1 (CLAUDE.md), ┬з10.1тАУ10.3, ┬з15, ┬з18.3; ADR-0001 (D5 swapped-
backbone integrity, D6 branch-B3 backbone fallbacks). Every version fact verified
against authoritative sources on **2026-07-08** (assistant cutoff is Jan 2026;
context7 ┬з3.3) тАФ a 6-agent research fan-out over PyPI JSON, `download.pytorch.org`,
GitHub release asset lists, the Caduceus HF config/modeling files, and the RiNALMo
paper.
**Process.** Authored `docs/decisions/ADR-0002-environment-and-ml-stack.md`
pinning D1 the per-env conda-lock policy (five envs, one lock each, **Python 3.12**
forced by numpy 2.5.1/scipy 1.18; the deprecated `pytorch-cuda` metapackage
replaced by a pinned `pip:` cu128 block тАФ a mechanism update within PRD ┬з15 intent,
flagged per precedence); D2 the load-bearing stack with a named version + rationale
per pin, gated by two ecosystem ceilings тАФ **`transformers` 4.57.5** (Caduceus
`trust_remote_code` unvalidated on v5) and the sm_86-kernel torch ceiling; D3 the
**`torch` 2.7.1+cu128 anchor** (Option B тАФ `mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3 source-built to
avoid the Mamba-3 Hopper-dep liability; `causal-conv1d` 1.6.2.post1 + `flash-attn`
2.8.3.post1 prebuilt), Option A all-prebuilt (torch 2.5) as the pre-registered
P0-06 fallback, Option C (Mamba-3) rejected, + the confirmed pure-PyTorch
selective-scan / SDPA fallbacks; D4 the CI Docker image; D5 the RiNALMo parity
numerics тАФ **N = ┬▒2 pp** (paper reports no per-split SD) with a telomerase carve-out
and a magnitude rationale, + the `multimolecule` 0.0.9 mirror pin + the env-split
contingency; D6 the RNA-FM fallback phasing (aтАУd) + the swapped backbone re-clearing
the **absolute GATE-1/GATE-2** gates and re-running the P4тЖТP5 go/no-go; D7 the
Caduceus two-part transfer go/no-go + continued-pretraining/NT-multispecies ladder
(ADR-0001 B3). D8 never-bump discipline; D9 delegations.
**Outputs.** `docs/decisions/ADR-0002-environment-and-ml-stack.md` (+ its embedded
cross-reference impact list). No DVC artifact (prose ADR).
**Validation.** Every load-bearing pin carries a named version + rationale тЬУ;
RiNALMo parity margin **N = 2 pp** numeric тЬУ; the two-part Caduceus go/no-go + the
RNA-FM-swap re-clearing conditions written per ┬з10.2/┬з10.3/┬з2.3 тЬУ; **user sign-off
recorded 2026-07-08** тЬУ (CLAUDE.md ┬з7 item 2, Option B as-is). CodeRabbit
zero-finding prose-diff gate on this PR (prose-only ADR + `.qmd` тЖТ expected zero
findings, correct scoping not a missing review).
**Decisions.** Anchor chosen for *correctness + reproducibility over recency*: torch
is set by the kernel ecosystem, not by torch itself; the newest `mamba-ssm` 2.3.x
(Mamba-3) was rejected because its hard `quack-kernels`/`tilelang`/`apache-tvm-ffi`
deps are Hopper/Blackwell-oriented and of unverified Ampere-import-safety тАФ a
load-bearing liability for zero Caduceus (Mamba-2) benefit. N=┬▒2 pp grounded in the
RiNALMo paper's 0.01 reporting granularity + same-weights seed/reimpl noise, with the
0.12-F1 telomerase outlier carved out. Cross-ref ADR-0002; parity cite `PMID:40593636
/ DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60872-5 / PMC12219582` (accessed 2026-07-08).
**Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-04 atomic commit on branch `p0-04-adr-0002`).

## Step P0-05 тАФ 2026-07-08: Build the five conda-lock environments (per-env lockfiles)
**Inputs.** ADR-0002 D1 (per-env conda-lock policy) + D2/D3 (the pinned stack).
`conda-lock` 4.0.2 (installed as an isolated dev env тАФ git-lfsтЖТ`~/.local/bin`
precedent; not a member of any `envs/*.yml`).
**Process.** Authored the five `envs/*.yml` specs (`conda-lock lock -p linux-64`
per-env, never aggregate; channels conda-forge[/bioconda][/nvidia]). Every pin
traces to ADR-0002 D2; the `viz`/`app` toolchains (ADR-delegated pins) were selected
and conda-forge-version-verified at build time. Locked + installed + import-smoked
the four CPU envs. The **`ml` lock is deferred to P0-06** (user decision 2026-07-08):
under the signed-off Option-B anchor `mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3 is source-only (no
torch-2.7 wheel; sdist carries no static metadata) so it needs `nvcc` + `sm_86` тАФ
absent on the laptop (`sm_89` GPU, no nvcc); P0-06 is its ADR-native home (the sm_86
wheel/build-availability check). `envs/ml.yml` is authored in full as the spec P0-06
locks. Added `tests/unit/test_env_manifest.py` (stdlib-only) and excluded the
machine-generated `*.conda-lock.yml` from CodeRabbit.
**Outputs.** `envs/{data,infernal,viz,app}.yml` + their `*.conda-lock.yml` (real
solves тАФ `data` 305 pkgs); `envs/ml.yml` (lock тЖТ P0-06);
`tests/unit/test_env_manifest.py`; ADR-0002 **Amendment A1**; `.coderabbit.yaml`
(`!**/*.conda-lock.yml`). No DVC artifact (env specs + locks are git-tracked).
**Validation.** `conda-lock install` reproduced all four CPU envs + import-smoke
PASS тАФ infernal (INFERNAL 1.1.5, ViennaRNA 2.7.2, Bio 1.87, numpy 2.5.1); data
(snakemake 9.23.1, dvc 3.67.1, polars 1.42.1, pandas 2.3.3, scikit-learn 1.9.0,
numpy 2.5.1); viz (matplotlib 3.11.0, seaborn 0.13.2, quarto 1.9.38); app (node
26.4.0 / npm 11.17.0, confirmed by absolute-path check тАФ the login-shell smoke's
v22.22.2 was an nvm-PATH artifact, not the env). `pytest tests/unit` 7/7 тЬУ; ruff
check + format тЬУ. The `ml` kernel import/forward smoke is deferred to P1 (cluster
GPU, ADR-0002 D3). Pins match ADR-0002 (data pandas 2.3.3 per Amendment A1).
**Decisions.** Two stop-and-asks resolved with user sign-off. (1) **`ml` lock
deferred to P0-06** rather than front-running P0-07's Docker image or a ┬з9.3 cluster
job. (2) **`data` pandas 3.0.3 тЖТ 2.3.3** тАФ bioconda `snakemake-executor-plugin-slurm`
2.7.1 over-caps `pandas<3.0` (contradicting the same version's upstream PyPI `<4`);
the pure-conda downgrade to the max 2.x keeps the load-bearing numpy-2.5.1 ABI and
forgoes only non-load-bearing pandas-3.0 features (polars 1.42.1 is the dataframe
workhorse). Recorded as ADR-0002 **Amendment A1** + a D2 pin-coupling bump-trigger;
`viz` pandas aligned to 2.3.3 for repo consistency. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D1/D2/D3 + A1.
**Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-05 atomic commit on branch `p0-05-envs`).

## Step P0-06 тАФ 2026-07-08: sm_86 wheel-availability check + pinned pure-PyTorch fallbacks
**Inputs.** `envs/ml.yml` (the Option-B kernel pins: torch 2.7.1+cu128, `mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3,
`causal-conv1d` 1.6.2.post1, `flash-attn` 2.8.3.post1, cp312/sm_86); ADR-0002 ┬зD3 (the wheel +
fallback claims to empirically confirm).
**Process.** Authored `scripts/check_sm86_wheels.py`, a stdlib PyPI-JSON + GitHub-Releases-REST
metadata probe (no GPU) that classifies each kernel `wheel-present`/`source-build-required`/
`unavailable` at the two D3 torch anchors, and ran it (token-forced GitHub). Independently
confirmed each torch-2.7 wheel is a real downloadable asset via a Range-GET probe, ran a 3-agent
adversarial verification, and read the raw `mamba-ssm` v2.2.6.post3 source to settle the fallback
question; context7 confirmed the `mamba` `use_fast_path` knob + the `multimolecule` RiNALMo
HF-attention path. Amended ADR-0002 (A2) and re-pinned `envs/ml.yml` to the three release-asset
wheel URLs.
**Outputs.** `scripts/check_sm86_wheels.py`; `tests/unit/test_check_sm86_wheels.py` (10 cases);
ADR-0002 **Amendment A2**; `envs/ml.yml` (three kernels pinned by exact release-asset URL, nvcc
re-scoped to DeepSpeed-JIT, lockfile note reversed тЖТ laptop-lockable). No DVC artifact.
**Validation.** Gate PASSED тАФ all three kernels **wheel-present at Option B** (torch2.7/cu12/cp312/
linux, abiTRUE): `flash-attn` (256 MB), `causal-conv1d` (194 MB), `mamba-ssm` (533 MB, 8,877
downloads). `pytest tests/unit` 16/16; ruff + black clean. The GPU import/forward smoke stays P1.
**Decisions.** The probe **disproved** D3's premise that `mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3 must be
source-built (**C1**: a prebuilt cu12torch2.7 wheel exists тЖТ Option B needs zero source builds,
`ml.yml` pinned by URL, P0-05 ml-lock deferral reversed to laptop-lockable) and **disproved**
D3's pure-PyTorch selective-scan fallback (**C2**: the packaged `Mamba` bare-imports
`selective_scan_cuda`; `use_fast_path=False` calls the CUDA kernel, not `selective_scan_ref`, so
a missing kernel is a capability loss тАФ only `causal_conv1d`тЖТ`nn.Conv1d` is speed-only; P1 must
assert `import selective_scan_cuda`). RiNALMo SDPA/eager fallback confirmed (**K1**). User
sign-off recorded 2026-07-08. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D3 + **A2**. Verification: artifact-based (probe
+ download-confirm + 3-agent adversarial pass + raw-source read).
**Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-06 atomic commit on branch `p0-06-sm86-wheels`).

## Step P0-06c тАФ 2026-07-08: split the `ml` env into `ml-dna` + `ml-rna`, lock both
**Inputs.** The P0-06b `ml.yml` (the proven torch 2.7.1+cu128 16-URL closure + three sm_86
kernels); ADR-0002 D2/D3/D5 + A2; the two P0-06b findings carried over (conda-lock 4.0.2 cannot
lock the cu128 stack via an index; `multimolecule` 0.0.9 needs transformers 5.x).
**Process.** Confirmed via PyPI `Requires-Dist` + the conda-forge 5.13.0 recipe run-deps + the
transformers v5.0.0 release notes (context7 ┬з3.3, plugin server; claude.ai server quota-blocked)
that transformers **5.13.0** (newest) caps `huggingface_hub<2.0,>=1.5.0` / `tokenizers<=0.23.0,>=0.22.0`
/ `safetensors>=0.8.0`, and that `multimolecule` 0.0.9 (uploaded 2026-02-02, after transformers
5.0.0) declares no transformers cap but imports `transformers.initialization` at runtime. Authored
`envs/ml-dna.yml` (= old `ml.yml` minus `multimolecule`, transformers 4.57.5) and `envs/ml-rna.yml`
(ml-dna + exactly three forced HF-core changes: transformersтЖТ5.13.0, hubтЖТ1.22.0, safetensorsтЖТ0.8.0,
plus `multimolecule` 0.0.9; tokenizers stays 0.22.2 since 0.23.0 is absent on conda-forge and 0.23.1
exceeds the cap). Both carry the identical URL-pinned cu128 closure. Locked both (`conda-lock -p
linux-64`), `conda-lock install`-reproduced both on the laptop (no GPU/nvcc), CPU import-smoked both.
Deleted `envs/ml.yml`; wrote ADR-0002 **A4** (folding in the retired A3 draft); extended
`tests/unit/test_env_manifest.py`.
**Outputs.** `envs/ml-dna.yml` + `envs/ml-dna.conda-lock.yml`; `envs/ml-rna.yml` +
`envs/ml-rna.conda-lock.yml`; `envs/ml.yml` **deleted**; ADR-0002 **Amendment A4**;
`tests/unit/test_env_manifest.py` (EXPECTED_ENVS тЖТ data/infernal/**ml-dna/ml-rna**/viz/app; both ml
locks required; torch-URL + no-`--extra-index-url` guard over both ml envs). No DVC artifact
(env specs + locks git-tracked).
**Validation.** Gate PASSED. Both `conda-lock` solve (ml-dna 52 s, ml-rna 72 s) + `conda-lock install`
reproduced locally with no GPU/nvcc; lockfile audit clean for both тАФ torch **2.7.1+cu128**, 14
`nvidia-*` + `triton` + the 3 kernels all at pinned URLs, **no `--extra-index-url` leak**. ml-dna
smoke: torch 2.7.1 CPU matmul + transformers **4.57.5** + `CaduceusConfig` via `trust_remote_code`.
ml-rna smoke: transformers **5.13.0** + `transformers.initialization` importable + **`import
multimolecule` + `RiNALMoModel` import/instantiate тАФ the P0-06b blocker, now cleared** (attn=eager,
SDPA-capable). `pytest tests/unit` 23/23; ruff + black clean. GPU kernel forward smoke (incl.
`import selective_scan_cuda`) + RiNALMo parity stay P1.
**Decisions.** Realized D5's pre-registered env split; the "multimolecule 0.0.9 era-matched to
4.57.5" premise (D2/D5) is **disproven** (needs v5). Minimize-divergence: ml-rna differs from ml-dna
in only three HF-core pins + multimolecule (accelerate 1.12.0 / peft 0.18.1 / datasets 4.5.0 already
admit hub 1.x + transformers 5). Durable operational finding: both envs bundle `cuda-nvcc` 12.8 for
DeepSpeed JIT and DeepSpeed probes `CUDA_HOME` at import (on the RNA side via
multimoleculeтЖТdanlingтЖТdeepspeed), so **runtime must export `CUDA_HOME=$CONDA_PREFIX`** (recorded in
A4; Snakemake/sbatch will set it). A3 draft never committed тЖТ folded into A4; no standalone A3.
User sign-off recorded 2026-07-08. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D5 + **A4**. Verification: artifact-based
(conda-lock solve/install logs + lockfile pin audit + 16-URL live probe + two CPU smokes).
**Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-06c atomic commit on branch `p0-06c-ml-split`).

## Step P0-07 тАФ 2026-07-08: Build the CI Docker image (CUDA 12.8 + PyTorch, GHCR)
**Inputs.** `envs/ml-dna.conda-lock.yml` + `envs/ml-rna.conda-lock.yml` (P0-06c), ADR-0002 D4 (as
amended by A2 C1 + A4). **Process.** Authored `docker/Dockerfile` (`docker/Dockerfile`) on the
`nvidia/cuda:12.8.1-cudnn-devel-ubuntu24.04` base (digest `sha256:24c8e358тАж`; latest 12.8 patch,
tag existence probed on Docker Hub), installing Miniforge **25.3.1-0** тЖТ `conda-lock` **4.0.2** (the
exact lock-generation version) тЖТ **both** locked envs via `conda-lock install --name <env> --mamba
--copy` (`--copy` avoids cross-layer hardlinks; `--validate-platform` default asserts linux-64). A
whitelist `.dockerignore` sends ONLY the two lock files into the build context (227 kB; no `.git`,
data, or secrets). An inline entrypoint activates `${TBOX_ENV:-ml-dna}` and exports
`CUDA_HOME=$CONDA_PREFIX` (ADR-0002 operational note). Built + smoked locally (BuildKit).
**Outputs.** `docker/Dockerfile`, `.dockerignore`; local image `tbox-finder:p0-07`
(`sha256:2c547f8fтАж`, **34.6 GB**). No DVC artifact (Dockerfile git-tracked; the image is a
CI/GHCR build artifact). GHCR push is wired to the CI schedule/release trigger in **P0-08** (D4:
built on schedule/release, not per-push). **Validation.** Gate PASSED. `docker build` exit 0; four
import smokes green тАФ two build-time (`conda run` per env) + two `docker run` end-to-end through the
entrypoint: **ml-dna** = torch **2.7.1+cu128** / `torch.version.cuda` **12.8** / transformers
**4.57.5**; **ml-rna** = same torch + transformers **5.13.0** + `import multimolecule` **0.0.9**
(the P0-06b blocker, exercised again in-container); the three prebuilt kernel wheels present in both
(`mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3, `causal-conv1d` 1.6.2.post1, `flash-attn` 2.8.3.post1, metadata-read тАФ the
GPU kernel *forward* smoke incl. `import selective_scan_cuda` stays P1, ADR-0002 A2 C2); entrypoint
sets `CUDA_HOME` to the active env prefix. **Decisions.** `-devel` base retained ONLY for DeepSpeed
JIT ZeRO ops (A2 C1 killed the mamba source build тЖТ zero source builds; `cuda-nvcc` 12.8 ships in
both conda envs). Installs BOTH locks per the A4 env split (the retired single `ml` lock is gone).
Entrypoint uses `set -eo pipefail` **without `-u`**: conda's `cuda-nvcc` activation
(`~cuda-nvcc_activate.sh`) references unset `NVCC_PREPEND_FLAGS`, which nounset aborts on тАФ a bug the
end-to-end `docker run` caught that the build-time `conda run` smoke masked. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D4 /
A2 C1 / A4. Verification: artifact-based (BuildKit build log + four container import smokes).
**Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-07 atomic commit on branch `p0-07-docker`).

## Step P0-08 тАФ 2026-07-08: GitHub Actions CI workflow (lint / DAG / tests / Docker)
**Inputs.** Repo scaffold (`pyproject.toml` tool configs, `workflow/Snakefile`, `src/tbox_finder`,
`tests/unit`), the committed `envs/data.conda-lock.yml` (+ ml-dna/ml-rna locks for the Docker job),
`docker/Dockerfile` (P0-07), ADR-0002 (env stack), the CLAUDE.md ┬з8.6 CI contract. **Process.**
Authored `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with four jobs. `lint` and `test` run on GitHub-hosted
`ubuntu-latest` via `actions/setup-python` 3.12 with pinned dev tools (`ruff==0.15.15`,
`black==25.11.0`, `pytest==9.0.2`) тАФ these live in no science env (the conda-lock/git-lfs
isolated-dev-tool precedent). `dag` reproduces the committed `data` env with
`conda-incubator/setup-miniconda` (Miniforge) + `conda-lock 4.0.2` (the exact lock-generation
version, mirroring `docker/Dockerfile`) and runs `snakemake --lint` + `snakemake -n` inside it
(`conda run -n data тАж`; snakemake 9.23.1, CLAUDE.md ┬з3.2 rule=env). `docker` builds + pushes the ML
image to GHCR, `if`-gated to `schedule`/`release`/`workflow_dispatch` only (never per-PR).
Added scaffold placeholder tests so `tests/{golden,ml}` collect тЙе1 test тАФ with unique module
basenames, since pytest's prepend import-mode collides on duplicate names when the dirs lack
`__init__.py`. **Outputs.** `.github/workflows/ci.yml`; `tests/golden/test_golden_scaffold.py`;
`tests/ml/test_ml_scaffold.py`. No DVC artifact (all git-tracked). **Validation.** Gate = CI green
on the PR that adds it. Pre-verified locally with the exact CI versions: `ruff check` exit 0,
`black --check` exit 0 (black 25.11.0), `pytest tests/{unit,golden,ml}` **25 passed**,
`snakemake --lint`/`-n` exit 0 in `tbox-finder-data` (snakemake 9.23.1). **Decisions.** ruff/black/
pytest pinned as isolated dev tools (not added to any science env), matching local practice + the
conda-lock/git-lfs precedent; snakemake stays science-pipeline tooling, run inside its pinned
conda-lock env. The `docker` job is schedule/release-only per ADR-0002 D4 / CLAUDE.md ┬з8.6; the
~34.6 GB image (P0-07) vs. a GitHub runner's disk is a flagged risk validated on the first
scheduled/dispatched build (a disk-reclaim step buys headroom), not on this per-PR gate. `push`
scoped to `branches-ignore: main` since `main` only receives squash-merges. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D4,
CLAUDE.md ┬з8.6 / ┬з3.2. **Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-08 atomic commit on branch `p0-08-ci`).

## Step P0-09 тАФ 2026-07-08: Author ADR-0003 тАФ Cluster & scan ops

**Inputs.** PRD ┬з7.2 (corpora, de-dup, sizing/feasibility lever, minimum-viable scan), ┬з15
(cluster topology, DVC-capacity rule, compute ceiling + patience budget, training/sweep budget,
shard-failure + node-outage rules), ┬з18.1тАУ18.3 (P0 deliverable, risks, ADR-0003 delegation line);
CLAUDE.md ┬з9 (cluster protocol); ADR-0001 (contract/precedence, format template).
**Process.** Authored `docs/decisions/ADR-0003-cluster-and-scan-ops.md` (D1тАУD10) pinning the
cluster/SLURM contract (D1: `gpu`/a4000, no `--account`/QOS/`--nodelist`/`compute`/`zero`,
artifact-based verification since `sacct` off), Snakemake-executor-CPU-only / GPU-via-hand-authored-
`sbatch` (D2), the estimate-anchored DVC-capacity rule (D3, concrete path/capacity forwarded to
P0-10), corpus de-dup `GTDB-rep > RefSeq-complete > MAG` (D4), corpus priority + minimum-viable
scan = tiers (1)+(2) + breadth-preserving feasibility lever (D5), the compute-ceiling determination
rule + patience budget (D6, numeric **frozen at the pre-P5 sizing gate**), the training/sweep
GPU-hour budget rule (D7, numeric frozen at P1 first measurement), and the two P0-numeric ops
rules. Draft was adversarially checked pre-sign-off by a 4-lens subagent sweep (PRD-faithfulness /
completeness / internal-consistency / validation-gate); 8 minor/nit cross-reference + rationale
fixes applied (blinded-freeze re-cited to ADR-0005, scan-plan-execution sub-gate to ADR-0006,
ADR-0004 added to Related/impact list, D4тЖФD5 corpus tiers aligned, D8/D9 magnitude rationales
added). **Outputs.** `docs/decisions/ADR-0003-cluster-and-scan-ops.md`
(sha256 `0a0bff87тАжc975d599f`, 125 lines).
**Validation.** Gate = compute-ceiling **rule** pinned with numeric explicitly deferred to the
pre-P5 sizing gate (D6) тЬУ; de-dup priority (D4) + minimum-viable-scan spec (D5) written тЬУ;
shard-failure fraction **тЙд 2%** (D8) + node-outage hold **72 h** (D9) numeric тЬУ; **user sign-off
2026-07-08** тЬУ тАФ **PASS**. **Decisions.** Two P0 numbers pinned as pre-registered *ops* thresholds
(D8 тЙд 2%; D9 72 h) тАФ not scientific-claim gates, so no ADR-0001-D4/ADR-0005 blinded-freeze, but
change-by-ADR-sign-off only; each threshold's sole failure mode is a CLAUDE.md ┬з7 stop-and-ask, so
both stay conservative. Compute ceiling + patience-budget days deferred to the sizing gate because
the binding number needs the P2 scanner's Stage-1 candidate density (PRD ┬з7.2/┬з15). Snakemake SLURM
executor deliberately barred for GPU (auto-retry would bypass the ┬з9.3 one-shot ack). Cross-ref
ADR-0001 (D3 non-circularity), ADR-0005 (recall floor), ADR-0006 (GATE-3 scan-plan-execution +
rollup); PRD ┬з7.2/┬з15/┬з18. No DVC artifact (prose ADR, git-tracked). **Commit.** (this commit тАФ the
P0-09 atomic commit on branch `p0-09-adr-0003`).

## Step P0-10 тАФ 2026-07-08: DVC init + dvc-ssh cluster-scratch remote + capacity pin & verify

**Inputs.** ADR-0003 D3 (estimate-anchored DVC-capacity rule); cluster scratch via `ssh two`;
`tbox-finder-data` env (dvc 3.67.1 + dvc-ssh 4.3.0 + asyncssh 2.23.0); the tboxevo `provenance.json`
convention (CLAUDE.md ┬з11). **Process.** `dvc init` (committed scaffold `.dvc/.gitignore` +
`.dvcignore`; `.dvc/config` and `.dvc/config.local` both stay gitignored per ┬з5.2). Added the
`dvc-ssh` remote `cluster-scratch` тЖТ `ssh://ecc1695@two/onedata/mondragonlab/ecc1695/tbox-finder/
dvc-remote` to per-machine `.dvc/config.local`. Discovered the scratch mount by `ssh two df`:
`/onedata` (shared beegfs, user-owned dir) was chosen over the 88 %-full HOME (`/scr/exports`, 691 GB
free) and over `/twodata` (19 TB free) for its shared-FS reachability from the `gpu` nodes + most
headroom. Wrote `src/tbox_finder/provenance.py` (`write_provenance` / `build_provenance` /
`sha256_file` / `git_sha` / `env_lock_hash`) + `tests/unit/test_provenance.py` (13 tests), wiring the
`env_lock_hash` that tboxevo had left a `null` stub. **Outputs.** committed scaffold; committed
capacity record `docs/dev-log/P0-10-dvc-capacity.md`; `src/tbox_finder/provenance.py` +
`tests/unit/test_provenance.py`; per-machine `.dvc/config.local` (uncommitted).
**Validation.** ADR-0003 D3 gate = `ssh two df` free **32.9 TB тЙе 2 TB provisional P5 estimate**
(тЙИ 16├Ч headroom) тЖТ **PASS**; `dvc remote list` shows the default remote; `dvc status` clean;
`provenance.json` carries all six ┬з11 stamps (rule / script / git-SHA / env-lock-hash / seed /
input+output sha256) тАФ asserted on real repo files; `pytest tests/unit` **36 passed** (23 + 13 new);
ruff + black clean; dvc-ssh **connectivity smoke** (throwaway `dvc push` round-trip through the
`zero`тЖТ`two` ProxyJump, object at `dvc-remote/files/md5/33/7bf06тАж`, then cleaned up) **PASS**.
**Decisions.** DVC-tracked artifacts exclude the multi-TB raw genome corpora (streamed to node-local
`/tmp`, discarded тАФ ADR-0003 D1/D3), so the 2 TB provisional budget covers only checkpoints +
interim/processed tables + training/eval datasets; the estimate is an *ops* engineering number (no
ADR-0005 blinded-freeze / no ┬з10.1 gate) and re-verifies when the pre-P5 sizing gate (ADR-0003 D6)
freezes the real volume. **Durable finding:** the encrypted `id_ed25519` breaks dvc-ssh if `keyfile`
is set (*"Passphrase must be specifiedтАж"*) тЖТ omit `keyfile`, rely on the ssh-agent (`allow_agent`
default), matching interactive `ssh two`. context7 ┬з3.3: dvc-ssh remote-config + ssh-config
resolution order confirmed (claude.ai Context7 quota-blocked тЖТ plugin server). **Commit.** (this
commit тАФ the P0-10 atomic commit on branch `p0-10-dvc-provenance`).

## Step P0-11 тАФ 2026-07-08: W&B offline scaffold + login-node `wandb sync` rule + `huggingface_hub` scaffold
**Inputs.** The locked `ml-dna`/`ml-rna` envs (wandb 0.28.0, huggingface_hub 0.35.3 тАФ ADR-0002); the
`conf/` Hydra groups; `workflow/Snakefile`'s auto-include of `rules/*.smk`; `src/tbox_finder/provenance.py`
as the bare-env-import style precedent. **Process.** Added `conf/tracking/wandb.yaml` (a Hydra
config-group option defaulting `mode: offline` тАФ SLURM `gpu` nodes have no reliable outbound for live
streaming, CLAUDE.md ┬з9.2/┬з11 тАФ with `project`/`entity: null`/`dir: wandb`, no secret); the first repo
`.smk` rule module `workflow/rules/setup.smk::wandb_sync` (login-node `wandb sync --sync-all {offline_dir:q}`,
`conda: ../../envs/ml-dna.yml`, `log:`, kept out of `rule all`); and `src/tbox_finder/hub.py` тАФ a release-time
(CLAUDE.md ┬з6.3) HF push/pull helper (`resolve_token`/`push_folder`/`pull_snapshot`/`push_model`/`push_dataset`)
that **defers** every `huggingface_hub` import into its functions so the module loads in a bare env, and
authenticates **only** from `HF_TOKEN`/`HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN` (token never logged/persisted). **Outputs.**
`conf/tracking/wandb.yaml`; `workflow/rules/setup.smk`; `src/tbox_finder/hub.py`; `tests/unit/test_hub_scaffold.py`
(5 guards: bare-env import, env-only token precedence, offline default, sync-rule shape, no committed secret).
**Validation.** All PASS тАФ `wandb sync --help` reachable from `tbox-ml-dna`; `mode: offline` is the compute-node
default; `test_no_committed_secrets` + secret-free files (no HF/W&B key literal); `import tbox_finder.hub`
succeeds in the base env with `huggingface_hub` **absent** (`pytest tests/unit` **41 passed**, 36 + 5 new);
`snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `snakemake -n` clean (first `.smk` rule lint-clean via `conda:` + `log:`);
`ruff` + `black` 25.11.0 clean. **Decisions.** Offline+login-node-sync (not the Snakemake SLURM executor) per
PRD ┬з16 / CLAUDE.md ┬з9.3 тАФ the executor auto-retries, bypassing the one-shot submit-ack. `hub.py` mirrors the
`provenance.py` deferred-import discipline so it imports in the bare CI test env (huggingface_hub lives only in
the `ml-*` envs). HF push is release-time only (┬з6.3); this is scaffold only, unwired from the DAG/training loop.
context7 ┬з3.3 (claude.ai Context7 quota-blocked тЖТ plugin server): wandb `sync --sync-all` + `WANDB_MODE=offline`;
huggingface_hub `HfApi.upload_folder`/`snapshot_download` + `HF_TOKEN` env auth (token not persisted). No DVC
artifact (all git-tracked). **Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-11 atomic commit on branch `p0-11-wandb-hf-scaffold`).

## Step P0-11a тАФ 2026-07-09: Stage the ┬з7.1 reference assets into `data/external/refs/`
**Inputs.** Immutable sibling laptop checkouts (PRD ┬з7.1 raw inputs, `sources_root=..`): `tboxdb-master/`
(class-I `RF00230.cm`; the four `master_datasets/MASTER/*_master.fa` тАФ the lowercase `*_master.fa` glob, which
excludes the uppercase `gecont3_full_MASTER.fa`), `tbox-scan-master/` (class-II `TBDB001.cm`, NAME `seed_ILE`),
and `tboxevo/data/processed/cms/` (the structural sub-element CMs `idtm_seq_aware.cm` / `idtm_structure_only.cm`
/ `idtm_structure_only.amendment2.cm` / `stem2_structure_only.cm`, and the 18-record class-II
`heldout_blind_set.fasta`). **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/refs.py::stage_refs` (stdlib-only; reuses
`provenance.sha256`/`git_sha`) copies each of the 11 assets, verifies it against a **pinned SHA-256** on both
source and copy (fail-loud, ┬з10.3), and writes `data/external/refs/provenance.json` carrying per-asset
source-path/version/accessed-date(2026-07-09)/license/sha256/bytes. Driven by the first `workflow/rules/data.smk`
rule `stage_reference_assets` (LOCAL, kept out of `rule all`; no `input:` тАФ sources are external; `dest_dir`
derived from `output.manifest` so `--lint` is clean; `conda: ../../envs/data.yml`). A `.gitignore` carve-out
`!/data/external/refs/**` commits the assets (git-LFS via `.gitattributes` `*.cm`/`*.fa`/`*.fasta`), while the
rest of `data/external/` stays ignored; `.snakemake/` + `/logs/` added to `.gitignore`. **Outputs.**
`data/external/refs/` тАФ 6 CMs + 5 FASTAs (all git-LFS) + `provenance.json`; `src/tbox_finder/refs.py`;
`workflow/rules/data.smk`; `tests/unit/test_refs_staging.py` (10 guards тЖТ 59 unit tests). **Validation.** PASS тАФ
`git lfs ls-files` lists all 11 CMs/FASTAs with LFS oids equal to their pinned sha256 (132-byte pointer blobs);
`provenance.json` is plain git (`filter: unspecified`); `git check-attr filter` == `lfs` on every `*.cm`;
`content_sha256` is LFS-pointer-aware so the checksum test passes in CI (no LFS smudge). `pytest tests/unit`
**59 passed**; `ruff` + `black 26.3.1 --target-version py312` + `ruff format` clean; `snakemake --lint` "good
condition" + `-n` clean. **Decisions.** `data/external/refs/` is the canonical committed home for these immutable
reference assets (┬з5.2 permits small committed CMs/FASTAs as git-LFS rather than DVC); the `*_master.fa` glob
stages all four MASTER FASTAs (RFGC3V = the combined RF00230/GC/gecont3/Vitreschak set + its three named
components) тАФ omission from an immutable dir is the costly error, and the PRD's descriptive "26,639 seqs" matches
no single file and is not this step's gate. context7 ┬з3.3 (plugin server): git-lfs v1 pointer spec
(`version`/`oid sha256:`/`size`) + `git check-attr filter` / `git lfs ls-files` verification. Consumed by P0-30
(masking/mining CMs), P4 (GATE-1 reference CMs), P6 (validation CMs + blind set). **Commit.** (this commit тАФ the
P0-11a atomic commit on branch `p0-11a-stage-refs`).

## Step P0-12 тАФ 2026-07-09: Data ingest + count/hash parse-correctness gate (23,535 ├Ч 107)
**Inputs.** `../tboxdb-master/Master_tboxes.csv` (raw, immutable; 92 MB, `wc -l` = 39,459 тАФ newline-inflated by
embedded newlines in quoted tRNA 2┬░-structure fields) and `../tboxevo/data/interim/master_clean_v0.parquet`
(the cleaned canonical corpus). **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/ingest.py::run_ingest` (driven by the new
`workflow/rules/data.smk::ingest_master`, LOCAL, out of `rule all`, no `input:` тАФ sibling sources external to the
DAG, like `stage_reference_assets`) does a proper RFC-4180 parse (pandas `read_csv`, no `index_col`) тЖТ asserts the
**parse gate 23,535 records ├Ч 107 raw columns** тЖТ reproduces the tboxevo cleaning contract *byte-for-byte* (drop
the leading unnamed pandas-export index column тЖТ 106 named cols; coord `0`тЖТNaN sentinel on 15 columns; `discrim_end`
`-2`тЖТNaN; `Regulation` value-set assertion, `Unknown` preserved) тЖТ appends a deterministic controlled-SHA-256
per-record `record_sha256` column тЖТ computes **per-record hash-identity vs the canonical parquet**. Verified once
that reading-without-`index_col`-then-dropping is value-identical to tboxevo's `read_csv(index_col=0)`
(`df.equals` True), so a single read serves both the 107-col gate and the 106-col clean. **Outputs.**
`data/interim/master_tboxes_ingested.parquet` (106 cols + hash, DVC md5 `7cbecf42тАж`); `data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet`
(106-col training corpus P1тАУP4 consume, DVC md5 `8356cf24тАж`); `data/processed/audits/count_parse_report.json`;
`data/interim/master_tboxes_ingested.provenance.json` (six ┬з11 fields; input paths normalized to the rule-relative
sibling refs, content-hashes authoritative); `src/tbox_finder/ingest.py`; `tests/unit/test_ingest.py` (+19: stdlib
hash-contract tier runs in bare CI, pandas tier `importorskip`); `tests/golden/test_ingest_golden.py` + the
100-record `tests/fixtures/ingest_sample/` (+ `expected.sha256`); replaced `tests/golden/test_golden_scaffold.py`.
**Validation.** **PASS** тАФ parse gate 23,535├Ч107 тЖТ 23,535├Ч106; **per-record hash-identity 100.0000% (23,535/23,535)**,
our_digest == canonical_digest `f471fdf6тАж`; golden fixture digest matches; parquet write is byte-deterministic
(stable DVC md5). `pytest tests/unit tests/golden` **79 passed** (data stack) / **68 passed + 11 skipped** in a
bare-pandas CI-test simulation (bare import of `ingest` works; stdlib tier green); `ruff 0.15.15` + `black 25.11.0`
(py311) + `ruff format` clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n` clean. **┬з20 open item (reported).**
class I:II = **22,334 : 1,200** (+1 unassigned) = **18.61 : 1** from the `type` column (Transcriptional=class I /
Translational=class II; PRD ┬з3, PMID:25583497); per-phylum: **Firmicutes 21,185 = 90.01%** dominant across
**29 phyla** (Actinobacteria 1,230; 453 NaN-phylum) тАФ feeds ┬з12's order-count claim + P0-26 power budget.
**Decisions.** Added **`pyarrow=24.0.0`** to `envs/data.yml` + regenerated `data.conda-lock.yml` (user-approved
env amendment, CLAUDE.md ┬з3.1; additive, no ML/stack-pin change тАФ all existing pins held): pandas+pyarrow is the
*only* path to a genuine 100% reproduction (an all-polars reimplementation diverged at 70.97% on float-format /
null-handling, and tuning it to pass would violate ┬з10.3). The per-record hash is a **controlled SHA-256** over a
canonical row serialisation, not `pandas.util.hash_pandas_object` (pandas-version-dependent), so the hash column +
golden digest are stable and human-auditable. **CodeRabbit (5 findings) тАФ 4 applied, 1 rejected:** the row-hash
encoding hardened to **kind-tagged + length-prefixed** tokens (collision-proof; a real string can't alias the missing
sentinel; `pd.NA` now hashes as missing, not `"<NA>"`); coord coercion made **fail-loud** on non-numeric junk
(┬з10.3; 0 hits on the corpus тЖТ output unchanged); report `passed` tightened to full cleaned shape. **Rejected:**
adding `discrim_end` to the zero-sentinel set тАФ the canonical has **0** `discrim_end==0` records and the tboxevo
contract deliberately excludes it (its sentinel is `-2`), so the change is a no-op that would deviate from the pinned
upstream contract; identity re-verified **100%** after the re-encode (only the interim hash column / digests changed,
processed md5 `8356cf24тАж` unchanged). **CodeRabbit re-review (2 further findings, both applied):** added an explicit
**column-schema gate** (a value-identical record under a renamed/reordered column can no longer pass тАФ
`columns_match` in the identity report) + **exotic-type hash disambiguation** (a non-str/float/int/bool cell is tagged
`S<type>:<str>` so it can't alias a string) тАФ both robustness-only, real-corpus digests + interim md5 `7cbecf42тАж`
**unchanged** (TBDB cells are str/float/int/bool). `verification: local (deterministic LOCAL rule; DVC push deferred to
phase-exit)`. context7 ┬з3.3: not required тАФ no new third-party library API written (pandas `read_csv`/`to_parquet`
reproduce the pinned tboxevo contract; pyarrow used only as the parquet engine). **Commit.** (this commit тАФ the
P0-12 atomic commit on branch `p0-12-ingest`).

## Step P0-13 тАФ 2026-07-09: Pin the governing GTDB release (+ GTDB-Tk ref-data package check, r220 fallback)
**Inputs.** tboxevo GTDB taxonomy maps (release pin); GTDB `release232/232.0` metadata (`MD5SUM.txt`, `stats/r232`);
GTDB-Tk reference-package index; `src/tbox_finder/provenance.py`. **Process.** A **Q-question stop** (CLAUDE.md ┬з7
item 1) resolved the release: two background investigations established that the tboxevo maps pin **GTDB R232**
(Release 11-RS232, 2026-04-15 тАФ tboxevo ADR-0001, cross-checked: GTDB's `bac120_taxonomy_r232.tsv` MD5 `4d42e137тАж`
== tboxevo's independent pin) and that R232 has an **available** GTDB-Tk package (`gtdbtk_r232_data.tar.gz`, тЙе 2.7.0),
so PRD ┬з7.2's contingency is satisfied and the **r220 fallback is not invoked**; user approved R232 + ADR-0003 A1
(2026-07-09). `src/tbox_finder/taxonomy.py::pin_release` (stdlib-only, bare-importable) then fetched + MD5-verified the
species-rep crosswalk `sp_clusters_r232.tsv` via `workflow/rules/data.smk::pin_gtdb_release` and ran the species-rep
count gate. **Outputs.** `data/external/gtdb/provenance.json` (committed via a `.gitignore` carve-out; release id +
base URL + GTDB-Tk package/version floor + CC-BY-SA-4.0 license + per-file authoritative MD5s + staged-crosswalk
SHA-256/bytes + published & counted species-rep values); the 49 MB `sp_clusters_r232.tsv` staged but gitignored
(re-fetched by the rule, ┬з5.2); genomeтЖТlineage taxonomy + metadata **pinned** (URL+MD5) for on-demand P0-14/15/22/P6
fetch; `src/tbox_finder/taxonomy.py` + `tests/unit/test_release_pin.py` + real `tests/fixtures/gtdb_sp_clusters/`
fixture.
**Validation.** **PASSED** тАФ species-rep count gate: counted `{total 199,923, bacteria 189,801, archaea 10,122,
other 0}` **==** GTDB-published `{199,923 = 189,801 + 10,122}` (fail-loud on drift, ┬з10.3); crosswalk MD5-verified
(`7e4b2fc2тАж`) + SHA-256 recorded; GTDB-Tk package available тЖТ r220 fallback recorded `false`. `pytest tests/unit`
**82 passed / 15 skipped** (bare CI-test sim тАФ taxonomy imports bare, ingest pandas tier `importorskip`); ruff 0.15.15
+ black 25.11.0 + ruff-format clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n` clean (rule out of `rule all`).
**Decisions.** Pinned **R232 over the r220 fallback** because its GTDB-Tk package exists (contingency met) тАФ matches the
sibling tboxevo release (zero ┬з13.2 cross-project crosswalk) and gives the largest, most complete novelty frame
(199,923 vs r220's 113,104 reps; a missing reference lineage is what yields a false "novel" call). Staged **only** the
species-rep crosswalk (row-count == the count gate); the ~120 MB genomeтЖТlineage tables are pinned-not-downloaded
(authoritative MD5 from GTDB `MD5SUM.txt`), fetched on demand тАФ keeps the pin step LOCAL-lean. ADR-0003 **Amendment A1**
(user sign-off 2026-07-09) fills the D10 release delegation; no locked decision changed. `verification: local
(deterministic LOCAL rule; no DVC artifact тАФ data/external is immutable/re-fetched, ┬з5.2)`. context7 ┬з3.3: not required
тАФ no third-party library API written (stdlib `urllib`/`hashlib` only). **Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-13 atomic
commit on branch `p0-13-pin-gtdb-release`).

## Step P0-14 тАФ 2026-07-09: Union novelty prior reconciliation + NCBIтЖТGTDB projection + unprojectable audit
**Inputs.** TBDB corpus `master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12, 23,535 records; NCBI phylum/class + `TaxId`, no
genome-assembly accession); `RF00230_master.fa` (P0-11a, 6,301 accessions); GTDB **R232** `bac120`+`ar53` taxonomy
TSVs (P0-13, fetched on demand + MD5-verified via `taxonomy.ensure_file`); the curated Vitreschak-2008 literature
occurrences (DOI:10.1261/rna.819308). **Process.** `workflow/rules/data.smk::reconcile_union_prior` тЖТ
`src/tbox_finder/priors.py` name-projects every record's NCBI lineage to its GTDB phyla via a curated
over-crediting base map (FirmicutesтЖТ`Bacillota*`; Proteobacteria routed by class тАФ ╬┤тЖТDesulfobacterota+Myxococcota,
╬▒/╬▓/╬│тЖТPseudomonadota; CPRтЖТPatescibacteriota; ChloroflexiтЖТChloroflexota; тАж), each candidate intersected with the real
R232 phylum universe (fail-loud on a fully-absent mapping); folds in the 622 RF00230-only masking loci (accession-only,
no taxonomy) and the 3 confirmed literature clades; re-derives the no-prior phylum list; runs a fail-loud
no-false-novelty gate (┬з10.3). Genome-resolution GTDB-Tk placement is deferred to P6 (┬з13.2) тАФ the corpus carries no
assembly accession, so P0-14 does the ┬з7.2 "finest-available (phylum) resolution" tier. **Outputs.**
`data/processed/priors/union_prior.parquet` (**DVC**, 24,160 records = 23,535 TBDB + 622 RF00230-only + 3 literature;
per-record source/accession/coords/NCBI-display-lineage/`gtdb_phyla_credited`/method) + `union_prior.provenance.json`;
`data/processed/audits/union_prior_report.json` (unprojectable audit + has-prior/no-prior phyla + the projection map +
literature evidence); `src/tbox_finder/priors.py` + `tests/unit/test_priors.py` (15 tests) + `tests/fixtures/priors/`.
**Validation.** **PASSED** тАФ accounting: 23,535 = **23,056 projectable + 479 unprojectable** (2.07% тЖТ 453 null-lineage
+ 24 eukaryotic mis-annotation + 2 obscure candidate-division; **map_gaps `{}`**); **23 has-prior / 174 no-prior phyla**
(R232 = 197); no-false-novelty gate green (8 known-T-box GTDB phyla тАФ Bacillota/Actinomycetota/Chloroflexota/
Deinococcota/Dictyoglomota/Desulfobacterota/Pseudomonadota/Synergistota тАФ all has-prior, none on no-prior; **no
`Bacillota*` on no-prior** so Clostridial T-boxes can't be mis-scored novel). `pytest` **96 passed / 16 skipped** (bare
CI sim тАФ priors imports stdlib-only, parquet tier `importorskip`) and **15/15** with pandas; ruff 0.15.15 + black
25.11.0 (`--target-version py312`) clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n` clean (rule out of `rule all`).
**Decisions.** **┬з10.1 evidence gate (Stops)** тАФ an 8-agent literature verification confirmed **3/4** Vitreschak clades
at тЙе2 mutually-independent sources (╬┤-proteoтЖТ**Desulfobacterota** [Geobacter/Pelobacter], *not* Myxococcota тАФ matches
the corpus's 38 Deltaproteobacteria rows, all Desulfuromonadales; DeinococcusтАУThermusтЖТDeinococcota; ChloroflexiтЖТ
Chloroflexota); **Dictyoglomi is single-source** (only Vitreschak 2008; FEBS-2010 + TBDB/Zhu-2021 omit it) тЖТ **WITHHELD**
from the curated set + flagged in the audit (**user decision 2026-07-09**, CLAUDE.md ┬з10.1) тАФ Dictyoglomota stays
has-prior via 10 TBDB corpus loci so the no-prior list is unchanged. Over-credit split taxa (safe: over-crediting costs
recall, under-crediting fabricates novelty тАФ ┬з7.2:163). ADR-0006 is **not yet authored** (P0-29); P0-14 builds against
the PRD ┬з7.2/┬з9.1/┬з13.3 spec directly (provenance `adr` = "PRD ┬з7.2 (ADR-0006 pending, P0-29)"). Two bugs caught +
fixed pre-commit: R232 CPR phylum is `Patescibacteriota` (not `Patescibacteria`) тАФ stranded 9 known records; and `class`
is a Python keyword so `itertuples`'s attribute access silently nulled every record's class (disabling the Proteobacteria
routing) тЖТ switched to positional-tuple iteration. `verification: local (deterministic LOCAL rule)`. context7 ┬з3.3: not
required тАФ no third-party library API written (stdlib + the in-repo `taxonomy`/`provenance` helpers; pandas lazy for the
parquet read only). **Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-14 atomic commit on branch `p0-14-union-prior`).

## Step P0-15 тАФ 2026-07-09: TaxId lineage re-placement for taxonomy-incomplete positives

**Inputs.** `master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12; NCBI-named lineage cols + per-record NCBI `TaxId`, 0 nulls) +
`master_tboxes_ingested.parquet` (row-aligned `record_sha256` hash-link, since master_clean carries no hash); a
**frozen, MD5-pinned NCBI taxdump snapshot** (`taxdmp_2026-07-01.zip`, NCBI `taxdump_archive`).
**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/taxonomy.py::replace_lineage` (+ `workflow/rules/data.smk::replace_taxid_lineage`, LOCAL,
out of `rule all`, lambda-derived path params) walks each incomplete record's TaxId lineage from the pinned taxdump
(`nodes/names/merged.dmp`, stdlib parse; merged-id redirect + cycle-guard) and **fill-only** recovers only the missing
ranks тАФ the curated 96% is never overwritten. Recovered labels are reconciled to the corpus's **pre-2021 NCBI vintage**
(`Firmicutes`, not modern `Bacillota`) using the **taxdump's own synonym / equivalent-name records** (no hand-built
rename map). **NCBI тАФ not GTDB тАФ is the re-derivation source** (Q-STOP resolved, user 2026-07-09): the TaxId is a native
NCBI id, the split groups on NCBI order strings, and GTDB is genome-keyed so it cannot resolve the environmental /
metagenome / CPR TaxIds that dominate the residue; GTDB R232 stays governing for novelty (P0-14) + P6 placement.
**Outputs.** `data/interim/lineage_replaced.parquet` (23,535 ├Ч 16 тАФ `record_sha256`, `TaxId`, `resolved_<rank>` +
`<rank>_source` тИИ {corpus, taxid_recovered, unresolved} for phylum/class/order/family/genus, per-rank
`dropped_from_{phylum,class,order}_holdout`, headline `dropped_from_clade_holdout` = order residue; DVC) +
`data/processed/audits/lineage_replacement_report.json` + `lineage_replaced.provenance.json` +
`data/external/ncbi_taxonomy/provenance.json` (taxdump pin: url/md5/sha256/license/bytes).
**Validation.** **PASSED** тАФ total accounting == **23,535** per rank (present + recovered + still_incomplete;
`accounting_ok`, fail-loud ┬з10.3); recovery per rank **phylum 32/453 (7.06%)**, **class 71/841 (8.44%)**, **order
59/928 (6.36%)** тАФ modest by design, the residue is genuinely-unplaceable environmental/metagenome + MAG-clade-only /
CPR taxa with no formal NCBI rank; **869 `dropped_from_clade_holdout`** (order residue тЖТ random-split-only) and the
load-bearing invariant holds: `resolved_order` NULL тЯ║ dropped, so **a no-clade record can never silently enter a clade
fold** (PRD ┬з9.2 / ┬з8.2). `pytest tests/unit` **116 passed / 5 skipped** (bare CI sim тАФ pure parse/lineage/reconcile
tier runs; parquet tier `importorskip` pyarrow) and **9/9** with pandas+pyarrow; ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0
(`--target-version py312`) clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n` clean.
**Decisions.** The **frozen governing-release taxonomy** phrase (PRD ┬з9.2) is realized as the **pinned NCBI taxdump**
for re-derivation, not GTDB тАФ a deviation from the P0-13-wired GTDB files, surfaced as a **┬з7 Q-STOP** (corpus is
NCBI-named at an old vintage + GTDB genome-keyed would corrupt split labels; **user chose Option A** тАФ pinned NCBI
taxdump + vintage-normalization). The 46 genuinely-**new recovered orders** are all Candidatus orders for organisms that
had no order before (not vintage-variants of existing corpus orders тЖТ no split incoherence), surfaced in
`new_taxa_introduced` for transparency. `fetch_taxdump` verifies MD5 vs the pin on `download=True` (production), trusts a
pre-staged file on `download=False` (offline/tests). ADR-0004 is **not yet authored** (P0-19); P0-15 builds against PRD
┬з9.2/┬з12 directly (provenance `adr` = "PRD ┬з9.2 (ADR-0004 pending, P0-19)"). context7 ┬з3.3: not required тАФ stdlib
(urllib/zipfile/hashlib) + in-repo `provenance` helper; pandas/pyarrow lazy for the parquet join only.
`verification: local (deterministic LOCAL rule)`. **Commit.** (this commit тАФ the P0-15 atomic commit on branch
`p0-15-taxid-lineage`).

## Step P0-16 тАФ 2026-07-09: Source the independent non-Firmicutes GATE-1 anchor (arm c)

**Inputs.** The **Vitreschak 2008** supplement (DOI:10.1261/rna.819308, PMID:18359782, PMC2271356) тАФ 3 open-access `.doc`
files (Table S1 abbrтЖТorganism crosswalk + Fig S1 T-box-leader alignment + legends), SHA-256-pinned, re-fetched not
committed (copyright); the training corpus `master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12, leakage baseline); the primary NCBI RefSeq
genomes of the confirmed hosts (via E-utilities). **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/anchors.py::source_anchor` (+
`workflow/rules/data.smk::source_gate1_anchor`, LOCAL, out of `rule all`, lambda-derived path param; stdlib-only import)
parses the gap-elided Fig S1 rows in **both** cp1251 + UTF-16LE (`strings -e s`/`-e l`, else the ╬┤-proteo/Deinococcus rows
silently drop) into contiguous genomic segments split by `(N)` omitted-length placeholders, resolves each host assembly тЖТ
RefSeq+GenBank replicons (`elink`), and **re-derives each leader from its primary genome by seed-and-extend localizing the
literature segments** (Vitreschak's sequence IS the query тАФ CM-free), requiring the inter-segment gaps to match the elided
lengths as the specificity guard, then extracting the full contiguous leader (elided middle filled from the genome). Each
host is GTDB-placed (name projection to the тЙе2-source-confirmed phylum; genome-resolution deferred to P6, matching P0-14)
and leakage-classified vs the corpus (RefSeq+GenBank accession-normalized, min/max-normalized coordinate overlap).
**Outputs.** `data/external/gate1_anchor/` (`.gitignore` carve-out; `.cache/` supplement+genomes re-fetched) тАФ
`gate1_anchor.fasta` (**16** re-derived non-Firmicutes leaders, git-LFS; headers carry accession:coords(strand)|organism|
phylum), `provenance.json`, `gate1_anchor_report.json` (raw counts per clade/host, GTDB placement, independence statement,
leakage report, withheld + unresolved).
**Validation.** **PASSED** тАФ **16 loci** across the 3 confirmed phyla (**Desulfobacterota 2, Deinococcota 5,
Chloroflexota 9**; 8 hosts), **12 exact + 4 approximate** (each with two-segment offset agreement); full provenance;
independence-from-RF00230 documented + the ascertainment-ceiling bound disclosed (reaches RF00230-cutoff-invisible but not
all-consensus-invisible); every host non-Firmicutes at **тЙе2 independent sources**; leakage report **11/16 corpus-overlap**
(TBDB scanned these hosts via `Vitreschak_master.fa` тЖТ held out at P0-24) + **5/16 coordinate-novel** (corrected тАФ see
**Review fix** below) + raw count reported
(the min-N / arm-(c)-fallback verdict is deferred to P0-26). `pytest tests/unit` **122 passed / 23 skipped** (bare CI sim
тАФ anchor: 17 pure-logic + import-purity run, 4 pandas-gated `importorskip`) and **21/21** with pandas+pyarrow; ruff
0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 (`--target-version py312`) clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n source_gate1_anchor`
valid.
**Decisions.** **┬з7 scientific-evidence-gate STOP resolved (user 2026-07-09):** approved **Option A** тАФ independent
organism-genome re-derivation (an 8-agent literature workflow established the supplement is accessible but carries **no
accessions/coordinates**, so loci are re-derived per organism+gene from the primary genome, localized by Vitreschak's own
Fig S1 sequence). **No env amendment** тАФ stdlib `urllib` E-utilities + subprocess `strings` suffice (Biopython, already in
`envs/data.yml`, not needed). **Dictyoglomus WITHHELD** (single-source тАФ only Vitreschak; MMBR-2009/FEBS-2010/TBDB-2021
omit тАФ reproduces the signed-off P0-14 decision); it stays has-prior via TBDB corpus loci but is never a certifying anchor
clade. The existing TBDB-derived `data/external/refs/Vitreschak_master.fa` was **rejected as the anchor** (CM-derived +
corpus-overlapping тЖТ exactly the circularity P0-16 exists to prevent). 2 *Dehalococcoides* `ileS` loci withheld as
**indel-divergent** (not gapless-localizable within tolerance; withhold-not-force-fit, ┬з10.3). ADR-0005 not yet authored
(P0-19/25) тЖТ builds against PRD ┬з7.1/┬з9.2(c)/┬з2.3 directly (`adr` = "PRD ┬з7.1/┬з9.2(c)/┬з2.3 (ADR-0005 pending, P0-19/25)").
context7 ┬з3.3: n/a (stdlib urllib/subprocess/hashlib + in-repo `provenance` helper; pandas lazy for the corpus read only).
`verification: local (deterministic LOCAL rule; NCBI E-utilities live-fetch)`.
**Review fix (CodeRabbit, pre-merge).** `localize_leader` returned minus-strand coordinates in **reverse-complement space**
(positions in `revcomp(genome)`), but they flowed unconverted into `classify_leakage` (compared against the corpus's
**forward-genome** coordinates), the audit `start_0based`/`end_0based_excl`, and the FASTA headers тАФ so the 6 minus-strand
loci were leakage-classified in the wrong coordinate frame (┬з8.2). Fixed to convert **both** strands' coordinates to the
forward genome (`[LтИТend, LтИТstart)`; the `leader` sequence is unchanged тАФ already sense). This flips **4** minus-strand loci
(**GSU_LEUA, DAC_LEUA, DG_ILES** [corpus stored under GenBank `CP000359`, invisible to a RefSeq-only lookup], **TR_LEUS**)
from coordinate-novel тЖТ **corpus-overlap**: each is present in the training corpus and must be held out at P0-24 тАФ a leakage
**miss** the buggy frame had masked. Leakage split corrected **7/16 тЖТ 11/16** overlap, **9 тЖТ 5** coordinate-novel; FASTA
leader sequences are byte-identical (only headers/coords change). Added a forward-coordinate unit invariant (asymmetric-flank
minus-strand planting, which a symmetric planting hid) + a Tier-2 end-to-end minus-strand-leakage-caught regression.
**Review fix 2 (CodeRabbit re-review, user-approved "apply all 4").** Four localization/reporting hardening findings on the
anchor: **F4** `source_anchor` now **fails loud** if zero loci localize (no silent empty artifacts, ┬з10.3); **F3** replicon
selection is ranked by localization quality (mismatches, then exact offsets тАФ `n_segments_matched` is invariant) and a tie
between **distinct** genomic locations (within a genome or across replicons) is **withheld**, not resolved by iteration
order (┬з10.3); **F1** the leakage report no longer counts a locus with a same-replicon corpus record that lacks coordinates
as coordinate-novel (`n_novel_no_overlap` тЖТ `n_no_confirmed_coord_overlap` + new `n_uncoordinated_same_replicon` /
`n_coordinate_novel`); **F2** the leader boundary is now derived from each segment's **actual matched position** (extended
across the short flanks by nominal spacing) instead of pinning the whole leader to one anchor's nominal offset тАФ so a shifted
(indel) offset no longer mis-sets the boundary. Re-run (offline, cache-only) is **stable at 16 loci / 8 hosts / 12 exact + 4
approximate**, leakage **unchanged at 11/16 overlap** (no locus withheld as ambiguous; F1 `n_uncoordinated = 0` on this
data); **F2 shifts 3 approximate leaders' end coordinate by 1тАУ2 nt** (DR_GLYS2 тИТ1, DEH_TRPB +2, DE_TRPB тИТ2; starts and all
overlap classifications unchanged; `genome[start:end] == leader` invariant re-verified). +5 tests (boundary-tracks-shift,
ambiguous/unambiguous localization, uncoordinated-not-novel, fail-loud-on-empty).
**Commit.** the P0-16 atomic commit on branch `p0-16-gate1-anchor` (`db4c3fc`) + two review-fix commits (squash-merged).

## Step P0-17 тАФ 2026-07-10: additional independent non-Actinobacteria class-II positives + leakage-test
**Inputs.** Published non-Actinobacteria class-II (translational) occurrences (peer-reviewed literature); the 18-record
single-phylum Actinobacteria/ILE natural set (`data/external/refs/heldout_blind_set.fasta`); the training corpus
`data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet` (read only to enumerate P2 leads).
**Process.** A тЙе2-source literature survey (workflow `wf_4bc70b3f-4b5`: 7 discovery angles + adversarial verification over
Consensus/PubMed/Scholar-Gateway/bioRxiv) returned a **verified NEGATIVE**: the peer-reviewed literature documents class-II
(translational) T-boxes **only in Actinobacteria** (the reduced *ileS* system). Every non-Actinobacteria candidate (10 loci
surfaced from TBDB's truncated-Stem-I `type=Translational` flag) was **REFUTED** тАФ TBDB's own curated `Regulation` field is
`Unknown` for all, none has explicit translational-mode evidence, and TBDB annotates a terminator hairpin for them (so the
call is not terminator-absence-based). Per CLAUDE.md ┬з10.2/┬з10.3, `src/tbox_finder/anchors.py::source_classII`
(`workflow/rules/data.smk::source_classII_positives`, LOCAL, out of `rule all`) publishes an **honest empty positive set**
(raw count 0) + the тЙе2-source evidence chain, and catalogues the corpus's **40** CM/DB-derived non-Actinobacteria
`type=Translational` records **by reference** as P2 de-novo discovery **leads** (explicitly NOT positives тАФ ingesting them
would be the CM/DB circularity P0-16/P0-17 exist to break). User sign-off (Option "Withhold + catalogue leads"), 2026-07-10.
**Outputs.** `data/external/classII_positives/{classII_positives.fasta (empty; git-LFS leaves 0-byte files empty),
provenance.json, classII_report.json}`. Report: `raw_positive_count=0`; leads = 40 (Firmicutes 20, Deinococcus-Thermus 11,
`(unresolved)` 4, Chloroflexi 2, Ca. Saccharibacteria 1, Ca. Wolfebacteria 1, Synergistetes 1); highest-spread priority for
P2 = {Chloroflexi, Deinococcus-Thermus}; leakage report **n_positives=0 тЖТ nothing added to the P0-24 no-leakage test**.
Evidence chain (тЙе2 independent, in-repo-cited): Vitreschak 2008 (PMID:18359782) + MMBR 2009 (PMID:19258532) + TBDB 2021
(PMID:32882008) + Sherwood 2015 (PMID:25583497) + M. tuberculosis 2024 (PMID:39097611). New `tests/unit/test_classII_provenance.py`
(2 tiers: 5 bare-stdlib + import-purity, 2 pandas-gated end-to-end incl. the anti-circularity "never emit a lead as positive"
guard).
**Validation.** PASSED тАФ full provenance (6 ┬з11 stamps); positives non-Actinobacteria (vacuous, 0); leakage-tested (0 added);
**raw class-II counts reported** (min-N-reachability verdict deferred to P0-26/ADR-0005). `pytest tests/unit` 125 passed / 24
skipped (bare CI sim; classII 5 pure-logic + import-purity, 2 pandas-gated `importorskip`) + 7/7 with pandas+pyarrow; ruff
0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 (`--target-version py312`) clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n source_classII_positives`
resolves. **Decisions.** The absence of a natural non-Actinobacteria class-II positive is itself the scientific gap the project
proposes to close (no database ever ran a class-II model outside Actinobacteria TaxID:1760) тАФ any such element the project
reports must be a NEW de-novo finding (terminator absence + SD sequestration), not a literature citation; the class-II
anti-mimicry pillar (PRD ┬з5 mechanism 3) rests on the 18-record Actinobacteria set + the P2 construction-powered
synthetic-class-II recovery set, as the PRD already anticipates. Withhold-not-fabricate, reproducing the signed-off P0-14
(*Dictyoglomus*) / P0-16 (*Dehalococcoides*) precedents. ADR-0005 (min-N floor) pending (P0-19/25). context7 ┬з3.3: n/a
(stdlib + in-repo `provenance`/pandas-lazy corpus read). **Commit.** the P0-17 atomic commit on branch `p0-17-classII-positives`.

## Step P0-18 тАФ 2026-07-10: Dataset-license-compatibility audit
**Inputs.** Every upstream source feeding the training corpus / curated dataset (TBDB `Master_tboxes.csv`, Rfam RF00230,
tbox-scan `TBDB001.cm`, tboxevo sub-element CMs, 9 PDB crystals, the Vitreschak-2008 supplement, GTDB R232, NCBI RefSeq +
taxdump, the P0-16 anchor, the P0-17 class-II set); ADR-0001 ┬зD9 (release-license gating); PRD header / ┬з2.1 G-E / ┬з7 / ┬з16тАУ17.
**Process.** Verified each source's license against the **authoritative upstream on 2026-07-10** тАФ GitHub About/LICENSE for the
MIT sources (the local `tboxdb-master` / `tbox-scan-master` archives omit the LICENSE, so confirmed against
`github.com/mpiersonsmela/tbox` + `github.com/jamarchand/tbox-scan`); the wwPDB usage-policy for PDB CC0-1.0; EMBL-EBI for Rfam
CC0-1.0; the GTDB downloads page for CC-BY-SA-4.0; NCBI policies for RefSeq/taxdump public-domain тАФ and cross-checked the
source-carried `data/external/**/provenance.json`. Landed `scripts/reconstruct_encumbered.py` (stdlib-only `SOURCES` registry =
single source of truth + checksummed fetch/reconstruct stubs), `docs/license_audit.md` (per-source verdict table + resolution),
`tests/unit/test_license_audit.py`. **Outputs.** 11-source audit; verdicts {9 compatible, 2 encumbered}; the two encumbered
route to reconstruct-from-source тАФ `gtdb` (CC-BY-SA-4.0 share-alike; novelty-prior projection re-applied via
`src/tbox_finder/priors.py`, **not embedded**) + `vitreschak_supplement` (proprietary; anchor leaders re-derived from
public-domain RefSeq via `anchors.py::source_anchor`, supplement never shipped).
**Validation.** PASSED тАФ every upstream source carries a license + compatibility verdict; the release path (own-derived
CC-BY-4.0 + reconstruct script for encumbered sources) is decided + consistent with ADR-0001 ┬зD9. `pytest tests/unit` **157
passed / 24 skipped** (bare CI sim; license-audit 32 pure-stdlib, no pandas gate) тАФ was 125/24; ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0
(`--target-version py312`) clean; no `.smk` touched (DAG lint unaffected). **Decisions.** Q-question RESOLVED тАФ user sign-off
2026-07-10, **Option A**: curated dataset = own-derived fields (coordinates, labels, predictions, splits, calibration,
re-derived anchor, hash-links) under **CC-BY-4.0** + `reconstruct_encumbered.py` for the two encumbered sources (Option B,
embed-everything-under-CC-BY-SA-4.0, declined тАФ share-alike reduces reuse + clashes with the CC-BY-4.0 model weights). GTDB is
the **only** share-alike source, and the corpus lineage-by-rank columns are NCBI-named (P0-15, public-domain taxdump), **not**
GTDB тАФ so the split-assignment table (P0-23) carries no GTDB content; GTDB touches only the union novelty prior, which ships as
own-derived has-prior/no-prior booleans (facts) with the GTDB-name compilation reconstructed. Corrects the P0-11a provenance's
imprecise "see tboxdb LICENSE" (the local archive has none; MIT confirmed upstream). context7 ┬з3.3: n/a (stdlib urllib/hashlib +
in-repo provenance reads). **Commit.** the P0-18 atomic commit on branch `p0-18-license-audit`.

## Step P0-19 тАФ 2026-07-10: Author ADR-0004 (Split & leakage policy)
**Inputs.** Measured all-pairs element-overlap prevalence on `data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12; 23,535 records; DVC md5 `8356cf24`, sha256 `1eb76591тАжb062dd2`) via new `scripts/measure_overlap_prevalence.py`; PRD ┬з8 (precedence), ┬з9.2 (splits/leakage), ┬з2.3+┬з12 (GATE-4); P0-15 taxonomy-incomplete residue policy; P0-16/P0-17 arm-(c) + class-II sets.
**Process.** Mapped the 7 annotated elements to their `(start,end)` columns, computed all 21 pairs' overlap prevalence + directional containment (inclusive-nt, per-record local frame, `-1`/NaN = absent, min/max-normalised for strand), persisted to `data/processed/audits/overlap_prevalence_report.json`. Authored `docs/decisions/ADR-0004-split-and-leakage-policy.md` (D1тАУD7): the single-label total precedence order + the measured table; the D2 structure-aware clustering cut; D3 clade-crossing; D4 taxonomy-incomplete; D5 nested single-checkpoint fold; D6 GATE-4; D7 no-leakage CI.
**Outputs.** ADR-0004 (Accepted); `scripts/measure_overlap_prevalence.py`; `data/processed/audits/overlap_prevalence_report.json`. **Three material overlaps** (18 others тЙд0.13% тЖТ disjoint): **SpecifierтКВStem_I 100.0%** (23122/23122, med 3bp), **AntiterminatorтИйTerminator 99.54%** (23332/23439, med 11bp), **DiscriminatorтКВAntiterminator 100.0%** (23208/23208, med 4bp). PRD's "98.3%" reconciled = specifier *presence* rate (23142/23535); conditional containment is total.
**Validation.** PASSED тАФ precedence table complete (all 21 pairs) with measured prevalence; GATE-4 тЙе0.80 floor + P0 magnitude rationale + ╬┤-recalibration-by-re-sign-off + blinded-freeze written; D2 clustering distance/coverage cut + sensitivity-sweep + histogram adequacy net pinned; **user sign-off 2026-07-10**.
**Decisions.** New-ADR sign-off (┬з7 item 2, user 2026-07-10). Two blinded-frozen pinned numbers: D2 clustering cut = **dтЙд0.30 / identityтЙе0.70 / coverageтЙе0.70** (single-linkage, CM-consensus-column distance) тАФ chosen over tighter (0.80/0.80) / looser (0.60/0.70) as the safe over-merge direction without multi-order mega-clustering; D6 GATE-4 = **min per-nt class F1 over {Stem I, Specifier, Antiterminator} тЙе 0.80**. Total precedence order `Discriminator тЦ╕ Specifier тЦ╕ Antiterminator тЦ╕ Terminator тЦ╕ Stem_II тЦ╕ Stem_III тЦ╕ Stem_I тЦ╕ background` resolves every overlap; pseudoknot folded into Stem_II by class definition (not precedence). Clustering-via-CM-alignment is split-only тЖТ not a discovery circularity (that lives at ┬з13.3 de-novo MSA, ADR-0006). context7 ┬з3.3: n/a (stdlib argparse/json/hashlib; pandas/numpy lazy for the parquet measure).
**Commit.** the P0-19 atomic commit on branch `p0-19-adr-0004`.
